title: Confirm your address registration timing
whyItMatters: Several admin steps depend on having a registered address; knowing your appointment or process date helps you sequence the rest.
suggestedNextStep: Book or confirm your municipality registration appointment and note the date you expect to be registered.

title: Confirm whether your employer or landlord needs a Dutch bank account within your first month
whyItMatters: Payroll and rent often require a local account; clarifying this sets the right order for your first weeks.
suggestedNextStep: Ask HR and your landlord or agency when they need your Dutch account details.

title: Confirm when your Dutch health insurance must begin and whether your work situation affects the timing
whyItMatters: Insurance timing can influence risk and month-one decision pressure; missing the deadline can lead to backdated premiums.
suggestedNextStep: Check with your employer or insurer when coverage must start, and note the last date to take out a policy.

title: Confirm which of your next admin steps cannot move forward until your BSN is active
whyItMatters: Bank account, DigiD, health insurance, and payroll often need a BSN; knowing the order avoids repeated delays.
suggestedNextStep: List the services you need in the first month and check which require a BSN; plan their order accordingly.

title: Check whether your employer supports relocation admin steps
whyItMatters: Employer support can reduce the time and complexity of early tasks such as registration, 30% ruling, and payroll.
suggestedNextStep: Ask HR for a relocation or onboarding checklist and whether they offer any support (e.g. registration letter, tax forms).

title: Confirm school and childcare registration and key dates in your municipality
whyItMatters: Family timelines often include additional lead-time and paperwork; early clarity avoids last-minute stress.
suggestedNextStep: Contact your municipality or school board and the chosen school or daycare for registration steps and deadlines.

title: Decide whether you want Dutch language learning, community building, or both as your first integration goal
whyItMatters: Realistic goals make language and community efforts sustainable and easier to plan.
suggestedNextStep: Choose one primary focus (e.g. a course or one regular activity) and block time for it in your calendar.

title: Confirm how long your current housing setup is stable
whyItMatters: Housing uncertainty can change priorities in month 2 and 3; knowing your contract or notice period helps you plan.
suggestedNextStep: Read your contract for end date and notice period; if temporary, decide when you will start looking for the next place.

title: Identify unresolved tasks from earlier weeks
whyItMatters: A catch-up audit prevents old tasks from undermining current planning and helps you prioritise what to do next.
suggestedNextStep: List every admin task you started but did not finish; pick the top 3 to complete in the next 2 weeks.

title: Identify which key documents are still missing
whyItMatters: Document gaps usually create repeated delays across registration, banking, and other tasks.
suggestedNextStep: Check our documents guide and list what you still need; order or request them as soon as possible.
